title: nlink-lab proc-stat --json output
description: Single-sample resource snapshot for a process inside a lab node, emitted by `nlink-lab proc-stat <LAB> <NODE> <PID> --json`. With `--watch`, one such object is emitted per line (NDJSON) at the requested interval. Built from /proc/<pid>/stat, /proc/<pid>/status, and /proc/<pid>/fd/ — read from inside the target namespace via nlink-lab exec.

host_pid.description: Host-side PID. Equal to ns PID today (no CLONE_NEWPID).

command.description: Short process name from /proc/<pid>/stat's comm field — first 16 bytes of the binary's basename, or whatever prctl(PR_SET_NAME) set. Never truncated to less.

uid.description: Effective UID of the process (column 2 of /proc/<pid>/status's Uid: line).

rss_kb.description: Resident set size in kB (VmRSS). Null for kernel threads / processes with no MM.

vsz_kb.description: Virtual address-space size in kB (VmSize). Null for kernel threads.

fd_count.description: Number of file descriptors open by the process.

cpu_user_ticks.description: User-mode CPU time in clock ticks since process start (utime). Convert to seconds by dividing by sysconf(_SC_CLK_TCK), typically 100.

cpu_kernel_ticks.description: Kernel-mode CPU time in clock ticks (stime).

started_at_unix_micros.description: Process start time as Unix epoch microseconds. Computed from starttime (jiffies since boot) + btime (from /proc/stat) + the system tick rate.

state.description: Single-character process state per `man 5 proc`: R running, S sleeping, D uninterruptible disk sleep, Z zombie, T stopped, t tracing-stopped, X dead, I idle.
